original: Aron Ralston, American mountaineer and motivational speaker (survived canyoning accident by cutting off arm), born in Marion, Ohio
First operation to separate conjoined twins joined at the head in India completed in New Delhi
1st Pali Road completed in Hawaii (winds so strong streams flow UP!)
Otto Wichterle, Czech chemist and inventor of the soft contact lens, born in Prostjov, Moravia (d. 1998)[1]
"ET" released to home video (14 million presold)
Water skis patented by Fred Waller
Islanders score 2 goals within 6 seconds 3 goals within 44 seconds
Benjamin O. Davis Jr. becomes the first African-American general in the United States Air Force.
DuPont announces its new synthetic polyamide fiber will be called "nylon"
Long-lost waltz byFrédéric Chopinreported rediscovered after 200 years at the Morgan Library & Museum in Manhattan

Alain Bombard, French biologist, physician, and politician who sailed across the Atlantic Ocean in a small boat to test his theory that a shipwrecked person could survive without provisions, born in Paris (d. 2005)
Samuel Williams and the first U.S. astronomical expedition to record an eclipse of the sun observes the event at Penobscot Bay
Great Britain issues postage stamp on 100th anniversary of JRR Tolkien
Tiger Woodsscores a wire-to-wire 3-stroke win over Hideki Matsuyama at the inaugural Zozo Championship at Accordia Golf & Country Club, tyingSam Snead's record of 82 PGA victories
China announces its population has reached over 1 billion people
Wes Studi is the first Native American actor to receive an Oscar, an honorary award for career achievement
American Dave Gryllis sets world bicycle speed record of 94.37 kph
Washington State University's running back Rueben Mayes sets collegiate football record of 357 yards rushing in one game (at Oregon)
Australian 7-year old mare Winx wins unprecedented fourth consecutive Cox Plate at Moonee Valley in Melbourne; her record 29th-straight win and her 22nd Group 1 racing success
NASA launches space vehicle S-200
1st Saturn launch vehicle makes an unmanned flight test
US Navy forms as the Continental Navy
Walt Disney's 1st TV show, "Disneyland", premieres on ABC
"Rebel Without a Cause", directed by Nicholas Ray, starringJames DeanandNatalie Wood, is released
Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, is founded by EnglishmanWilliam Penn
First section of New York subway opens, running from Lower Manhattan to Broadway Harlem for a fare of one nickel, built by Interborough Rapid Transit (IRT)
Golden Gate National Recreation Area created
Niccolò Paganini, Italian violin virtuoso, and composer (24 Caprices for Solo Violin; Princess Lucca), born in Genoa, Republic of Genoa (d. 1840)[1]
Roy Lichtenstein, Pop art painter (painted comic book panels), born in New York City (d. 1997)
Tamla/Motown Records releases "Talking Book", the 15th studio album byStevie Wonder;includes the hit singles "Superstition", and Grammy Awrd-winning "You Are the Sunshine of My Life"
American pop and soul singer Ben E. King records "Spanish Harlem" and "Stand By Me" in NYC
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelwins the Indian Grand Prix at the Buddh International Circuit to clinch his 4th straight F1 World Drivers Championship; 6th consecutive GP victory
AmericanSerena Williamswins her 4th season ending tennis title; beatsLi Naof China 2–6, 6–3, 6–0 in the WTA Championship final in Istanbul, Turkey
Mary T. Meagher, American swimmer (3 x Olympic gold 100/200m butterfly, 4x100m medley 1984; WR 100/200m butterfly 1981), born in Louisville, Kentucky
Vanessa-Mae [Vanakorn Nicholson], Singaporean-British violinist and Olympic alpine skier, born in Singapore
French runner Chantal Langlacé sets female world marathon record (2:46:24) in Neuf-Brisach, France
Detroit Red Wings right wingGordie Howescores 544th career goal in 6-4 loss to Montreal Canadiens, tying Maurice Richard's all-time NHL regular season record
Taylor Swiftreleases her 5th studio album "1989" (2015 Billboard Album of the Year, Grammy Award for Album of the Year 2016)
American soccer starAbby Wambachannounces her retirement
Joseph Medicine Crow, Native American chief and historian, born near Lodge Grass, Montana (d. 2013)
Ruby Dee (née Wallace), American Emmy Award-winning stage and screen actress (A Raisin in the Sun; American Gangster), poet, and civil rights activist, born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2014)
Federalist Papers start appearing in New York newspapers under pseudonym "Publius" (written byAlexander Hamilton,James Madison, andJohn Jay)
Republic of Congo-Kinshasa becomes Republic of Zaire
Hertogenbosch & Tilburg freed from nazi occupation
Royal Australian Navy announces discovery of wreck of a World War II submarine (likely Japanese) in Simpson Harbour, Papua New Guinea during Operation RENDER SAFE
Soviet submarine S-363 runs aground near the Swedish naval base at Karlskrona in the "Whiskey on the Rocks" incident
Roman EmperorConstantine the Greatis said to have received his famous Vision of the Cross
Icelandic writerHalldór Laxnessis awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature
Terry J. Hart, American astronaut (STS 41C), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Michael Baker, Lt Cmdr USN/astronaut (STS 43, 52, 68, 81), born in Memphis, Tennessee
Missy Mazzoli, American classical composer (Breaking the Waves; The Listeners), and pianist, born in Lansdale, Pennsylvania
Tanel Padar, Estonian singer-songwriter (46th Eurovision Song Contest winner, 2001 - "Everybody"), born in Uhtna, Estonia
Kristi Richards, Canadian freestyle skier (FIS World C'ship gold Moguls 2007, bronze 2011), born in Penticton, British Columbia
Irfan Pathan, Indian cricket all-rounder (29 Tests, 1 x 100, HS 102, 100 wickets, BB 7/59; 120 ODIs; Baroda, Middlesex CCC, Kings XI Punjab, Delhi Daredevils), born in Vadodara, Gujarat, India
Jess Carter, English soccer defender (Birmingham, Chelsea, NJ/NY Gotham FC), born in Warwick, England
Simon Le Bon, English rock vocalist (Duran Duran - "Hungry Like The Wolf"), born in Bushey, Hertfordshire, England
Anthony Doerr, American writer (All the Light We Cannot See - Pulitzer Prize for Fiction 2015), born in Cleveland, Ohio[1]
Maxine Hong Kingston, American author (The Woman Warrior), born in Stockton, California
Philip Catherine, Belgian jazz guitarist (Chet Baker; Stephane Grappelli; Niels-Henning Ørsted Pedersen; Jean-Luc Ponty), born in London, England
Steven R. Nagel, American astronaut, USAF (STS 51-G, 61-A, 37, 55), born in Canton, Illinois (d. 2014)
